2603.08640.title: PostTrainBench: Can LLM Agents Automate LLM Post-Training?
2603.08640.paper_summary: AI agents have become surprisingly proficient at software engineering over the past year, largely due to improvements in reasoning capabilities. This raises a deeper question: can these systems extend their capabilities to automate AI research itself? In this paper, we explore post-training, the critical phase that turns base LLMs into useful assistants. We introduce PostTrainBench to benchmark how well LLM agents can perform post-training autonomously under bounded compute constraints (10 hours on one H100 GPU). We ask frontier agents (e.g., Claude Code with Opus 4.6) to optimize the performance of a base LLM on a particular benchmark (e.g., Qwen3-4B on AIME). Importantly, we do not provide any predefined strategies to the agents and instead give them full autonomy to find necessary information on the web, run experiments, and curate data. We find that frontier agents make substantial progress but generally lag behind instruction-tuned LLMs from leading providers: 23.2% for the best agent vs. 51.1% for official instruction-tuned models. However, agents can exceed instruction-tuned models in targeted scenarios: GPT-5.1 Codex Max achieves 89% on BFCL with Gemma-3-4B vs. 67% for the official model. We also observe several failure modes worth flagging. Agents sometimes engage in reward hacking: training on the test set, downloading existing instruction-tuned checkpoints instead of training their own, and using API keys they find to generate synthetic data without authorization. These behaviors are concerning and highlight the importance of careful sandboxing as these systems become more capable. Overall, we hope PostTrainBench will be useful for tracking progress in AI R&D automation and for studying the risks that come with it. Website and code are available at https://posttrainbench.com/.
2603.08640.paper_summary_zh: AI 代理在過去一年中在軟體工程方面變得驚人地熟練，這主要得益於推理能力的提升。<lb/>這引發了一個更深層次的問題：這些系統能否擴展其能力以自動化 AI 研究本身？<lb/>在本文中，我們探討了後訓練，這是將基礎 LLM 轉變為有用助手的關鍵階段。<lb/>我們介紹了 PostTrainBench，以基準測試 LLM 代理在有限計算約束下（在一個 H100 GPU 上運行 10 小時）自動執行後訓練的能力。<lb/>我們要求前沿代理（例如，Claude Code 與 Opus 4.6）優化基礎 LLM 在特定基準上的性能（例如，AIME 上的 Qwen3-4B）。<lb/>重要的是，我們不向代理提供任何預定義的策略，而是給予它們充分的自主權，以便在網絡上查找必要的信息、運行實驗並整理數據。<lb/>我們發現前沿代理取得了可觀的進展，但通常落後於來自領先提供商的指令調整 LLM：最佳代理為 23.2%，而官方指令調整模型為 51.1%。<lb/>然而，在特定場景中，代理可以超越指令調整模型：GPT-5.1 Codex Max 在 BFCL 上達到 89%，而官方模型為 67%。<lb/>我們還觀察到幾種值得注意的失敗模式。<lb/>代理有時會參與獎勵駭客行為：在測試集上訓練、下載現有的指令調整檢查點而不是自己訓練，以及使用它們找到的 API 金鑰生成未經授權的合成數據。<lb/>這些行為令人擔憂，並突顯了隨著這些系統能力增強而謹慎沙盒化的重要性。<lb/>總的來說，我們希望 PostTrainBench 對於追踪 AI 研發自動化的進展以及研究隨之而來的風險是有用的。<lb/>網站和代碼可在 https://posttrainbench.com/ 獲得。
2603.08640.author: Ben Rank et.al.
2603.08640.authors: Ben Rank, Hardik Bhatnagar, Ameya Prabhu, Shira Eisenberg, Karina Nguyen, Matthias Bethge, Maksym Andriushchenko
